content.problemStatement:
After solving programming problems for years, Chef has become lazy and decided to get a better physique by doing some weight lifting exercises.

On any regular day, Chef does NNN exercises at times A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ (in minutes, all distinct) and each exercise provides a tension of B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ units. In the period between two consecutive exercises, his muscles relax RRR units of tension per minute.

More formally, Chef's tension is described by a number xxx. Before any workouts, x=0x=0x=0. When he does a workout at time AiA_iAi​, the tension xxx instantly increases by BiB_iBi​. Between workouts, the number xxx decreases by RRR units per minute, maximized with 000.

Considering the time of exercise and hence tension to be negligible, find the maximum tension he will be feeling in his muscles during the entire period of his workout.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains 333 lines of input.
The first line will contain 222 space-separated integers N,RN, RN,R, number of timestamps at which Chef performs his exercise, and units of tension relaxed per minute.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.
###Output:
For each testcase, output in a single line the maximum amount of tension Chef will have in his muscles.

###Constraints


1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤N≤5⋅1041 \leq N \leq 5\cdot 10^41≤N≤5⋅104
1≤R,Bi≤1051 \leq R, B_i \leq 10^51≤R,Bi​≤105
1≤Ai≤1091 \leq A_i \leq 10^91≤Ai​≤109
Ai−1<AiA_{i - 1} \lt A_iAi−1​<Ai​, for all 2≤i≤N2\le i\le N2≤i≤N

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1: Since there is only 111 exercise, the maximum tension is equal to the tension provided by that exercise, i.e, 101010 units.
Test Case 2: At time t=10t = 10t=10, Chef has 101010 units of tension.
From t=10t = 10t=10 to t=11t = 11t=11, his muscles releases 222 unit of tension and at t=11t = 11t=11, he further gains 101010 units of tension to have total of 10−2+10=1810 - 2 + 10 = 1810−2+10=18 units of tension.
So the maximum tension Chef feels in his muscles is 181818 units.
Test Case 3: At time t=1t = 1t=1, Chef has 111 unit of tension.
From t=1t = 1t=1 to t=2t = 2t=2, his muscles releases 111 unit of tension and at t=2t = 2t=2, he further gains 222 units of tension to have total of 1−1+2=21 - 1 + 2 = 21−1+2=2 units of tension.
From t=2t = 2t=2 to t=3t = 3t=3, his muscles releases 111 unit of tension and at t=3t = 3t=3, he further gains 333 units of tension to have total of 2−1+3=42 - 1 + 3 = 42−1+3=4 units of tension.
So the maximum tension Chef feels in his muscles is 444 units.
